Class ServerController

java.lang.Object
src.ocsf.server.AbstractServer
server.ServerController
All Implemented Interfaces:
Runnable
public class ServerController extends AbstractServer
Main server controller that extends the AbstractServer. It manages client connections and routes incoming messages to specific command objects using a Command Pattern implemented with a HashMap.

  • Field Summary

    Fields
    Modifier and Type
    Field
    Description
    private HashMap<ActionType, Command>
    commands
    A map linking each ActionType to its corresponding Command execution logic.
    private Map<ConnectionToClient, String>
    connectedClients
    A thread-safe map storing active client connections and their descriptive info strings.
    static final int
    DEFAULT_PORT
    The default port number used by the server if none is specified.
    private ServerUIController
    ui
    Controller for managing the Server's User Interface.

  • Field Details

    • commands

      private HashMap<ActionType, Command> commands
      A map linking each ActionType to its corresponding Command execution logic.

    • DEFAULT_PORT

      public static final int DEFAULT_PORT
      The default port number used by the server if none is specified.
      See Also:
      • Constant Field Values

    • ui

      private ServerUIController ui
      Controller for managing the Server's User Interface.

    • connectedClients

      private Map<ConnectionToClient, String> connectedClients
      A thread-safe map storing active client connections and their descriptive info strings.

  • Constructor Details

    • ServerController

      public ServerController(int port)
      Constructs a new ServerController on the specified port. Initializes the command map and registers all supported application commands.
      Parameters:
      port - the port number to listen on.

  • Method Details

    • setUi

      public void setUi(ServerUIController ui)
      Associates a UI controller with the server to display connection updates and logs.
      Parameters:
      ui - the ServerUIController instance to use.

    • clientConnected

      protected void clientConnected(ConnectionToClient client)
      Triggered automatically when a new client connects. Logs connection details and updates the Server UI.
      Overrides:
      clientConnected in class AbstractServer
      Parameters:
      client - the connection object representing the connected client.

    • clientDisconnected

      protected void clientDisconnected(ConnectionToClient client)
      Triggered when a client disconnects normally.
      Overrides:
      clientDisconnected in class AbstractServer
      Parameters:
      client - the connection that was closed.

    • clientException

      protected void clientException(ConnectionToClient client, Throwable exception)
      Triggered when a client connection is lost due to an exception.
      Overrides:
      clientException in class AbstractServer
      Parameters:
      client - the connection that encountered the error.
      exception - the throwable cause of the disconnect.

    • handleDisconnection

      private void handleDisconnection(ConnectionToClient client, String type)
      Internal helper method to process client removals from the tracking map and UI. Ensures that disconnection logic is only executed once per client.
      Parameters:
      client - the client being disconnected.
      type - a string describing the nature of the disconnect.

    • initializeCommands

      private void initializeCommands()
      Populates the commands HashMap with ActionType keys and their respective Command implementations. This maps client requests to server logic.

    • handleMessageFromClient

      protected void handleMessageFromClient(Object msg, ConnectionToClient client)
      Core message handler for all incoming data from clients. It expects a Message object, extracts the ActionType, and executes the corresponding Command found in the map.
      Specified by:
      handleMessageFromClient in class AbstractServer
      Parameters:
      msg - the object received from the client (expected to be of type Message).
      client - the connection object for the client that sent the message.
